title: The Return
pov: Dual limited third — Jaina Proudmoore in Scenes 1-3; Sylvanas Windrunner in Scenes 4-5
timelinePosition: Opens hours after Chapter 21's retreat from the Frostmourne cavern and closes at the palace approach in Capital City.
openLocation: The road south toward Muradin's camp in Dragonblight.
transport: Casualty carry on foot -> camp handoff -> return march to Daggercap Bay -> southbound crossing aboard the Wolfheart -> immediate landfall at Southshore -> overland race on the King's Road -> final approach on foot toward the palace.
immediateObjective: Get the cave intelligence off Northrend, turn it into a workable anti-blade plan, and reach Capital City before the kingdom's lie hardens completely around Arthas.
constraints: Limited Third Absolute — Jaina in Scenes 1-3, Sylvanas in Scenes 4-5.
No off-POV interior access; state shifts must be shown through behavior, movement, timing, and observable cost.

formationKnowsAtOpen: Arthas claimed Frostmourne and the prince they chased is gone in the only useful sense.
Muradin is alive enough to carry but badly wounded.
Frostmourne's field contains a seam - an oscillation point between consuming and radiating phases.
The Wolfheart is still the only usable route back south.
No warning is going to matter unless it is fast, brutal, and simple.
formationDoesNotKnowAtOpen: Whether Muradin survives the handoff to his camp.
How much of Arthas's return can still be interrupted politically.
Whether any warning sent from the bay reaches someone in time to matter.
Whether Jaina can turn the oscillation point into something usable under live combat pressure.
How deeply Arthas has already settled his people inside the palace by the time they reach Lordaeron again.
mustNotBeImpliedYet: No fully complete throne-room choreography before the road-planning beats earn it.
No clean promise that Antonidas's arrival, Uther's access, or any institution can still stop Arthas from outside the room.
No claim that Jaina can narrow the widened sensitivity once this chapter finishes using it.
No tribunal logic yet; that belongs to the aftermath chapter.
castKnowsLeavingChapter: Muradin is handed to Baelgun's camp alive enough to keep.
The warnings go out by every available lane and fail in practice.
Arthas has been in Capital City for four days before the formation reaches the walls.
Jaina's return push is faster than the voyage north and more damaging.
The oscillation finding becomes a three-school framework and then a physical choreography.
Sylvanas is the named combat partner who must buy the spell its seconds.
Antonidas reaches Capital City with two Kirin Tor magi, but their help stops at the palace margins.
Uther is in the palace but still cannot reach the king in time by formal means.
Cyndia's protective sweep now covers Jaina as well as her older lane.
The formation reaches the palace with no public alarm and no useful witness guaranteed.

jaina.currentStanceAtOpen: The cave loss only matters if she can turn what she heard there into a different kind of weapon.
jaina.surfaceObjective: Extract the formation, get the warning south, and turn the seam in Frostmourne's field into a framework someone else can actually help execute.
jaina.pressureSource: Muradin's weight in the snow, Arthas's lead, the louder bread-taste, the widened channel, and the fact that every hour spent being thorough is an hour the prince gets closer to the throne room.
jaina.misbeliefManifestation: Jaina still wants the finding itself to be the answer, as if seeing the seam clearly enough could bridge the distance between understanding and delivery.
jaina.chapterTest: The chapter asks whether she can stop treating intelligence as self-sufficient and admit it only matters if it can survive in another person's hands and body.
jaina.forcedChoice: Spend herself to get home fast and keep thinking, or conserve herself and arrive with more reserves but no kingdom left to save.
jaina.endStateShift: Jaina moves from holding a flaw in the blade to holding a framework that only matters if someone else buys her time to use it.

sylvanas.currentStanceAtEntry: Political rescue is over. If there is still a way to matter, it lives inside the room where Arthas stands, not in any court or council around it.
sylvanas.surfaceObjective: Deliver Jaina to the throne room in castable condition and turn the anti-blade framework into a live engagement plan before they reach the palace doors.
sylvanas.pressureSource: Jaina's depletion, the kingdom's festive ignorance, the lack of allies who can act, and the knowledge that the final plan spends Sylvanas's own body as part of its machinery.
sylvanas.misbeliefManifestation: Sylvanas still wants the problem to become tactically neat if she plans hard enough, even though politics has already made it dirty beyond repair.
sylvanas.chapterTest: The chapter asks whether she can stop leaving 'someone' abstract and become the combat partner in practical terms rather than in implication.
sylvanas.forcedChoice: Keep the role language vague, or let the planning itself expose the fact that she is the one who must stand in Frostmourne's reach.
sylvanas.endStateShift: Sylvanas moves from co-investigator to the person who will physically hold the death knight in place while the mage works.

team.label: partnership
team.operatingModeAtOpen: Post-recognition under retreat strain - warning, analysis, motion, no spare language.
team.operationalChangeThisChapter: The partnership moves from 'we know the problem' to 'we have a real answer only if both bodies do their part.'
team.doNotOverstateRule: Let warning failure, permitted overreach, pronoun shift, and final threshold carry the change. Do not narrate romance or sacrifice ahead of page proof.

entries.rule: Blunt dwarven certainty. He takes a wounded commander, says what the camp can do, and pushes them back toward the coast.
Pragmatic, sharp, and highly competent. She reads strain like a captain working with a dangerous engine she did not build.

Already in Capital City by the final approach with two Kirin Tor magi and no real authority left. He can hand them a side lane, latest palace status, and one last human look. He cannot stop the room by formal means.
Ordinary, hospitable, and catastrophically uninformed. They are not fools; they are living inside the kingdom's lie.
Hot, efficient, and all bad news.
Carry work, relay discipline, concealment, and the proof that the formation still protects its own under pressure.

castAndHandoffRules.chapterHandoffTarget: Chapter 23 opens with service-passage infiltration, undead royal guard, Uther's near-miss, and the throne-room plan finally under live pressure.

scenes.title: The Handoff
scenes.sceneFunction: Strip the cave down to its immediate cost and force the return south to begin before grief, warmth, or argument can slow it.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: The formation got out clean only by retreating fast.
scenes.arcPressure: Keep Muradin's body, dwarven camp labor, and Jaina's shaking hands visible so the chapter starts in aftermath rather than in theory.

enrichment.detail: Muradin's Frostmourne warning lands harder if the handoff keeps him in his canon role: Arthas's old combat mentor and the first body the blade punishes. Dragonblight / Frostmourne-approach space should feel like improvised expedition country — cold, stripped, and practical enough that Baelgun's answer is treatment plus immediate dismissal back to the road.
enrichment.source: Local: stories/the-plague-road/characters/muradin.md, stories/the-plague-road/locations/lordaeron-and-northrend.md; Web: Wowpedia - Muradin Bronzebeard, Warcraft Wiki - Dragonblight

beats.goal: Get Muradin to people who can keep him alive and learn whether the formation can stay without being swallowed by the same danger that wrecked the cave fight.
beats.conflict: The camp can treat Muradin; it cannot solve Frostmourne or keep S&J safe if they linger.

outcome.summary: Baelgun takes Muradin and promises the camp will hold him, but the handoff comes with the blunt truth that S&J need to leave now and carry the warning themselves.

beats.valueShift: clean retreat -> responsibility narrowed to the pair who understand it
newOnPageInformation: Muradin is alive enough for treatment, even if nothing more can be promised yet.
Baelgun reads the danger as something that can come looking for them if they stay put.
The camp keeps Muradin; the investigation keeps the burden of warning the south.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether Muradin wakes in time to matter later.
Whether any warning can beat Arthas home.
sensoryAnchors: dwarven hands taking Muradin's weight without ceremony
blood freezing dark in beard braids and on fur trim
Baelgun sounding angrier than shocked because anger is the only useful shape left
beats.transitionIntent: Once Muradin is handed off, the chapter can let Jaina sit with what the cave actually gave her.

beats.goal: Leave the camp before comfort, grief, or debate can slow the return.
beats.conflict: The camp offers a pocket of warmth, but warmth here is just delay dressed kindly.

outcome.summary: The formation turns back toward the coast immediately, but they leave behind the only shelter they have seen in days and carry no guarantee that the race south is still winnable.

newOnPageInformation: No one wastes time pretending a fuller recovery window exists.
Light's Vengeance and the cave both remain behind them, unresolved.
Jaina leaves still half inside the fight because the field's rhythm has not left her head.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether the return crossing can be made faster than the voyage north.
Whether Jaina's head is carrying a weapon or only the ghost of one.
sensoryAnchors: camp heat falling away after a few strides
Muradin gone from view behind palisade timber
Jaina's fingers already tracing the same invisible pattern as they walk
beats.transitionIntent: The chapter now has room to stay inside the march long enough for the oscillation point to become more than panic memory.

scenes.title: The Return March
scenes.sceneFunction: Turn cave residue into the first real anti-blade intelligence and make the cost of carrying that intelligence bodily.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: Jaina is carrying something from the cave she cannot yet name cleanly.
scenes.arcPressure: Keep march rhythm, cold, shaking hands, and the bread-taste active so the analysis never floats free of the body doing it.

enrichment.detail: Dragonblight canon supports a windswept dragon graveyard under Icecrown pressure, not a generic snowy road. The return leg should keep the cold as ambient hostility, with the Frostmourne approaches reading like ancient bone-country and glacial fallout still hanging on the formation's bodies.
enrichment.source: Local: stories/the-plague-road/locations/lordaeron-and-northrend.md; Web: Warcraft Wiki - Dragonblight, Wowpedia - Frostmourne Cavern

beats.emotion: The mind refusing to let the cave collapse into failure because one piece inside it still rings true.
beats.dilemma: Jaina can hear the seam in Frostmourne's field clearly enough to know it matters, but not yet clearly enough to know what can pass through it.
beats.decision: She keeps working the rhythm in her head instead of turning the retreat into blank grief.
beats.valueShift: retreat begins -> the blade acquires a flaw
newOnPageInformation: Frostmourne's field is not one continuous state; it cycles between taking and throwing.
Jaina's widened channel is what let her hear the shift at all.
The cave loss left behind a timing problem rather than only a combat problem.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: What can exploit the seam.
Whether the seam exists long enough in each cycle to matter in a real fight.
sensoryAnchors: Jaina counting in her head against bootfalls in snow
bread-taste turning sharper each time the remembered cycle lines up
fingers writing phase marks in empty air because no paper is fast enough
beats.transitionIntent: Once the flaw exists, the chapter can test what kind of certainty Jaina is building around it.

beats.dilemma: Jaina is doing one crucial thing exactly the way Kel'Thuzad did it - hearing elegance in a terrible mechanism and following that elegance toward mastery.
beats.decision: She does not stop thinking it through, but she does stop pretending the parallel is flattering or harmless.
beats.valueShift: the blade acquires a flaw -> the flaw gains moral pressure
newOnPageInformation: Jaina can finally see why Kel'Thuzad found the Lich King's work intellectually irresistible.
The same habits that solved the plague are now studying Frostmourne.
Being repulsed by a thing does not stop her from understanding it better than anyone else alive.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether the difference between Jaina and Kel'Thuzad is moral intent, outside friction, or something else still unearned.
Whether she can carry this line of thought without letting it hollow her out.
sensoryAnchors: glacier pressure still felt behind her even while the formation moves away
the remembered pitch-change in Frostmourne's cycle repeating under her skull
Sylvanas beside her as the silent proof that the thinking is not happening in isolation
beats.transitionIntent: After the echo beat, the body has to remind Jaina what this thinking is costing.

beats.emotion: The body's revolt against everything the last two chapters demanded of it.
beats.dilemma: Jaina can keep using the channel that is making her useful, but every use makes it more likely the channel stops being optional.
beats.decision: She keeps using it because the return requires intelligence more than comfort.
beats.valueShift: moral pressure -> the cost becomes bodily and permanent-feeling
newOnPageInformation: The bread-taste is louder after the cave than it was after the voyage north.
Jaina's tremor is now steady baseline, not merely post-casting aftershock.
Cyndia and the rangers sense enough of the change to treat Jaina's lane as protected without saying so.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether the channel ever narrows again.
Whether the price of hearing the blade matters first in the fight or only in the aftermath.
sensoryAnchors: yeast-warm smell rising from Jaina's skin in killing cold
fingers that never fully stop shaking
snow glare making exhaustion feel sharper instead of softer
beats.transitionIntent: Once the body has stated the cost, the chapter can get them back to the coast and test whether warnings matter at all.

scenes.title: Daggercap and the Crossing
scenes.sceneFunction: Spend every warning lane, prove how late they are, and turn the sea crossing into the price of a usable framework.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: The pair have a flaw in the blade's cycle, but no delivery method and no political cover.
scenes.arcPressure: Keep docks, pigeons, quartz stone, sail strain, food, and nosebleeds concrete so warning failure and breakthrough both feel paid for.

enrichment.detail: Southshore canon is a busy Alliance port town and road hub, which helps the failed landfall read as ordinary life continuing under the wrong story. The sea leg should keep ship labor material — tar, rope, cold spray, forced food, and sail handling — so speed feels purchased instead of narrated.

beats.goal: Reach the bay and get the ship turned around immediately.
beats.conflict: Every hour spent in harbor is an hour Arthas uses better than they can, and nobody on the quay can solve that.

outcome.summary: Dagny gets the Wolfheart ready on speed terms rather than comfort terms, but the harbor itself offers no answer beyond leave now.

newOnPageInformation: Dagny understands from one look that the southbound run must be immediate.
The bay still bears the scar of Arthas's passage and offers no new hope.
The return voyage will be faster only if Jaina is willing to pay harder than she paid northbound.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether the price of that speed leaves anything for the throne room.
Whether a warning launched from here can reach anyone meaningful in time.
sensoryAnchors: same harbor, now uglier because they know what left it
crew moving turnabout work at a captain's clipped command pace
Jaina looking at the water like distance has become a personal insult
beats.transitionIntent: Once the ship can sail, the chapter has to spend every message lane that still exists.

beats.goal: Send every warning available before the ship clears the bay.
beats.conflict: Every channel is structurally weak for a truth this large - birds can miss, stones can drain, institutions can believe and still fail to act.

outcome.summary: The warnings are sent, and the most important return intelligence says Arthas is already in Capital City.

beats.valueShift: urgency becomes logistical -> urgency becomes hopelessly late
newOnPageInformation: Sylvanas launches the warning through military shorthand and Farstrider signaling while Jaina reopens the Antonidas line one last time.
Antonidas confirms Arthas arrived home days ago and the court is celebrating rather than bracing.
The world believes the prince ended the threat instead of carrying it home.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether any single bird or reader acts against the momentum of celebration.
Whether arriving before the parade matters less than arriving before the blade feeds too deeply.
sensoryAnchors: carrier pigeons flinging themselves south over frozen water
amber light in the sending-stone dying in Jaina's hand before the conversation is finished
a flare or relay signal blooming uselessly beautiful against a sky too wide to answer
beats.transitionIntent: Once the warnings fail in practice, the only thing left is speed.

beats.goal: Get back to Lordaeron faster than the northbound voyage took.
beats.conflict: The southbound sea offers no supernatural wall, which means Jaina can push harder and damage herself more cleanly.

outcome.summary: The crossing compresses brutally, but the speed comes straight out of Jaina's reserves and leaves almost nothing unspent by landfall.

beats.valueShift: hopelessly late -> progress purchased at a harsher exchange rate
newOnPageInformation: The return run is faster because the source no longer cares enough to oppose them directly.
Dagny can tune the ship around Jaina's output with unnerving efficiency.
Cyndia's protective attention to Jaina becomes visible even without explanation once the bread-smell strengthens.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether Jaina arrives with enough left for the real fight.
Whether anyone ashore has used the warning intelligently.
sensoryAnchors: almost constant nosebleed by the end of the run
food forced into Jaina's hands because logistics outrank appetite
the Wolfheart running fast under a wind that now comes from one exhausted mage instead of from weather
beats.transitionIntent: Once the ship is moving south fast enough, the chapter can let the flaw become something closer to a weapon.

beats.emotion: Exhaustion sharp enough to strip away ornament.
beats.dilemma: The oscillation point is real, but a flaw is useless unless Jaina can imagine what actually passes through it and changes the blade from inside.
beats.decision: Jaina builds the first complete framework: frost to match the rhythm, arcane to penetrate the seam, fire to alter the binding from inside. It still needs delivery.
beats.valueShift: progress purchased at a harsher exchange rate -> the flaw becomes a working framework
newOnPageInformation: The weakness is not a break point; it is a transition point.
The answer requires three schools in sequence rather than one overwhelming spell.
The framework only matters if someone can buy Jaina three to five seconds within arm's reach of the blade.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Who actually gives her those seconds.
Whether Jaina's depleted body can cast the framework even once under combat conditions.
sensoryAnchors: night stars over a ship still carrying the day's push in its timbers
Sylvanas sitting beside Jaina because the finding now needs to become work
Jaina laying the sequence out in plain field language instead of Scholomance terms
beats.transitionIntent: Once the framework exists, the chapter can land them in Lordaeron and turn someone into a road-south problem.

beats.dilemma: Home soil should mean support; instead it confirms the warning died in the distance and the final leg has to be walked anyway.
beats.decision: South. Immediately. No pause longer than dockwork requires.
beats.valueShift: the flaw becomes a working framework -> the final movement begins
newOnPageInformation: Southshore is ordinary, calm, and fatally unwarned.
Jaina's collapse on stopping the push proves how little she has left under the skin.
The three-school framework survives the voyage intact enough to carry into planning.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether the framework can be translated into real choreography before they reach the palace.
Whether the kingdom can still be saved politically or only physically.
sensoryAnchors: Lordaeron earth feeling soft and almost warm after Northrend ice
Jaina's knees giving under her the moment she stops feeding the sails
Sylvanas's hand near enough to catch without actually taking Jaina's weight
beats.transitionIntent: The second half opens on the King's Road with garlands, a road-dirty kingdom, and a plan that still needs a body to carry it.

scenes.title: The Road South
scenes.sceneFunction: Push the formation through a kingdom celebrating its own danger, let the last formal lane collapse into one last human handoff, and get the pair to the capital with nothing left but the room.
castInScene: Antonidas by stone from Capital City
knowledgeAtSceneStart: Jaina's framework still needs a body to carry it.
scenes.arcPressure: Keep pace, road texture, autumn warmth, Jaina's condition, and the kingdom's public joy concrete so the chapter never floats into summary.

enrichment.detail: Pre-fall Lordaeron should stay fertile, trafficked, and harvest-dressed even while the protagonists know it is already doomed. The road south works best as civic normalcy weaponized against urgency: orchards, wheat stubble, village garlands, and unworried gate practice all proving the kingdom has mistaken celebration for safety.

beats.goal: Establish the formation's road pace and test how much of Jaina's body the final leg can still ask for.
beats.conflict: Every mile toward Capital City is necessary, and every mile burns reserves the throne room will need more.

outcome.summary: The formation holds a hard southbound pace, but Jaina's condition is now the limiting variable in every time calculation.

beats.valueShift: final movement begins -> Jaina becomes the limiting variable
newOnPageInformation: Lordaeron's warmth feels wrong after Northrend rather than comforting.
The formation's body positions have become automatic through weeks of co-work.
Sylvanas is now measuring the road in terms of what it costs Jaina, not just in miles.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether the throne room asks for more than Jaina has left.
Whether any help inside the city still exists.
sensoryAnchors: soft Lordaeron earth after weeks of ice
wheat stubble and autumn orchards beside a road no one has fortified
warm bread-smell hanging around Jaina at three paces
beats.transitionIntent: Once the pace is set, the kingdom's lie can become visible in the first village and the timeline can get even worse.

beats.goal: Pass through the first celebratory village without breaking cover and absorb the newest intelligence without stopping.
beats.conflict: Warning these people would cost time, cover, and probably the whole palace approach, but silence means walking straight through living innocence.

outcome.summary: The formation cannot speak the truth here, and the relay runner makes the timeline worse by confirming Arthas has already been installed in the palace for four full days.

beats.valueShift: Jaina becomes the limiting variable -> the kingdom's lie is fully installed
newOnPageInformation: Arthas's return has already become flowers, banners, and children's drawings.
Villagers speak about the prince as a hero who ended the threat rather than brought it home.
The palace still runs peacetime gates and visiting patterns while Arthas's imported guard force settles inside it.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: What the blade has fed on during those four days.
Whether open gates mean carelessness or absolute confidence.
sensoryAnchors: goldenrod garlands on fence posts and tavern doors
a hot brass message cylinder in a runner's hand
Sylvanas going still in the exact way she does when the math worsens
beats.transitionIntent: Once the timeline is fully bad, the chapter can test the last formal lane through Antonidas.

beats.goal: Get the final status from Antonidas and learn whether any court or alliance machinery has moved.
beats.conflict: Even the one man who believes Jaina is now fighting etiquette, sovereignty, and celebration from inside the capital itself.

outcome.summary: Antonidas confirms every formal lane failed, but he is already in Capital City with two Kirin Tor magi and can offer one discreet meeting at the palace margin, not a public solution.

beats.valueShift: the kingdom's lie is fully installed -> institutional failure confirmed
newOnPageInformation: The Kirin Tor filed concern after concern and then sent the smallest possible physical response when paper failed.
Antonidas believes Jaina enough to come in person with two magi who can independently feel the blade's wrongness.
Uther is physically in Capital City and still shut out by celebration, timing, and court insulation.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether Uther becomes ally, witness, or one more silenced truth.
Whether Antonidas can do anything useful once S&J reach the palace core.
sensoryAnchors: weak blue light from the stone in Jaina's trembling hands
Jaina's jaw tightening once and only once while she listens
blue stone-light showing Antonidas's face gone lean with travel and argument
beats.transitionIntent: Once Antonidas turns from distant voice into a body waiting at the palace edge, the chapter can spend its last movement on the plan itself.

scenes.title: The Plan and the Capital
scenes.sceneFunction: Turn Jaina's framework into combat timing, let Antonidas's last useful help end at the service door, name Sylvanas as the body that carries it, and move the formation inside without public alarm.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: No public lane is going to solve this, but Antonidas is waiting with the only discreet opening still left to them.
scenes.arcPressure: Keep side-court stone, palace residue, memory-map work, dawn fatigue, and Cyndia's sweep on page so the chapter lands through labor and motion rather than summary.

enrichment.detail: Canon palace / imperial-chamber material supports white marble, a gold floor seal, an octagonal dome window, balconies, and service infrastructure running behind ceremony. Antonidas and Uther being physically in Capital City at this moment diverges from Warcraft III canon, but the local story arc and chapter 23 handoff already establish that AU state, so it is preserved rather than corrected.
enrichment.source: Local: stories/the-plague-road/locations/lordaeron-and-northrend.md, stories/the-plague-road/plans/endgame-arc.md, stories/the-plague-road/plans/chapters/chapter23.md; Web: Wowpedia - Capital City, Lordaeron's Palace, Imperial Chamber

beats.goal: Meet Antonidas, learn the palace's live state, and secure an entrance that does not announce them.
beats.conflict: Antonidas has gotten himself and two Kirin Tor magi to the capital, but ceremony, sovereignty, and celebration make open intervention slower than murder.

outcome.summary: Antonidas confirms Uther is inside, gives them the best side lane left, and commits the Kirin Tor pair to holding outer attention and softening one service ward, but his help stops at the edge of the palace core.

beats.valueShift: institutional failure confirmed -> the palace gains a human edge
newOnPageInformation: Antonidas came in person once paper and stone both failed.
The Kirin Tor magi can independently feel the blade's wrongness from the palace margins.
No one with standing is willing to turn that wrongness into a public interruption before the room opens.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether the side lane stays clear long enough to matter.
Whether Antonidas or his magi become useful witnesses after the fact.
sensoryAnchors: blue-white Kirin Tor glow hooded under a service arch
Antonidas looking road-tired instead of tower-composed
palace stone already colder near the hidden court than the morning warrants
beats.transitionIntent: Once Antonidas's help is reduced to a doorway, the chapter can spend the real plan.

beats.goal: Translate the three-school anti-blade sequence into a physical choreography and stop pretending someone else will carry the cost.
beats.conflict: The plan requires Sylvanas to hold Arthas at close range inside Frostmourne's consuming field for three to five uninterrupted seconds.

outcome.summary: The choreography becomes clear enough to attempt, and the commitment turns from abstract to personal the moment Sylvanas says I, but the margin all three listeners can calculate is still too thin to call safe.

beats.valueShift: the palace gains a human edge -> Sylvanas becomes the named combat partner
newOnPageInformation: Frost syncs the rhythm, arcane breaches the seam, and fire changes the binding from inside.
Jaina must stand close, still, and fully committed while the cast runs.
Sylvanas is the person who has to hold the death knight's attention long enough for the spell to land.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether Sylvanas survives the drain long enough to hold the line.
Whether Jaina's reserves survive three schools under live conditions.
sensoryAnchors: Jaina speaking in plain field language because there is no room left for academy talk
dawn light catching on Antonidas's face when he hears the price of the plan
silence after three to five seconds because everyone present knows what that means at sword range
beats.transitionIntent: Once the role is named, the chapter can move that reality into the formation and through the door.

beats.goal: Enter the palace, read the final formation, and decide whether to wait one breath longer or go now.
beats.conflict: The city is festive, the guards are soft, and Antonidas's presence cannot travel with them without breaking the stealth the plan depends on.

outcome.summary: The formation enters through the service lane without challenge while Antonidas peels off to keep a public lane alive, and Sylvanas catches that Cyndia's protective sweep now covers Jaina as surely as it once covered Lyriel's lane.

beats.valueShift: Sylvanas becomes the named combat partner -> final threshold
newOnPageInformation: Parade residue still marks the approach roads and palace gate.
Guards are relaxed because the kingdom believes the danger is over.
Antonidas and the Kirin Tor cell will be in the palace margins, not in the room where anything can be proven.
Jaina still does not know she has inherited a dead ranger's coverage.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether Arthas expects intruders specifically or simply fears nothing left inside the walls.
Whether any outsider arrives soon enough to witness what really happens.
sensoryAnchors: WELCOME HOME, PRINCE ARTHAS over the gate
flower paste ground into the road by days of traffic
Cyndia's head turning through the same protective pattern while the service door closes behind them
beats.transitionIntent: Chapter 23 opens inside the service passages with the plan under live pressure at last.

warnings: Dual-POV contract risk: this chapter stays scene-split by POV, so scene-level POV labels were preserved explicitly for downstream agents rather than flattened to one chapter-wide interior lane.
Beat 16 was normalized from hybrid scene/sequel labeling to a scene beat because it runs on goal / conflict / outcome rather than emotion / dilemma / decision.
Beats 11-16 become a six-beat scene run once beat 16 is normalized, so the back half needs sharp differentiation between road pressure, institutional collapse, planning, and threshold entry.
Value shifts across beats 11-13 darken in one direction, which can flatten the road section into one long worsening unless each beat changes the pressure type.
Scene outcomes skew almost entirely yes-but / no-and, with no clean yes; the draft will need at least one strongly legible local win or the chapter may read as one long compromised grind.
